factors.name: Treasury Yields & Real Rates Rising
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports 'Treasuries Lead Global Bond Yields Higher on Inflation Angst' with persistently higher oil prices threatening inflation. Rising yields compress long-duration growth multiples — critical headwind for Nasdaq 100 mega-caps (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA, TSLA).

factors.name: Inflation Surprise (Japan PPI +4.9% vs +3.0% expected)
factors.summary: Japan's wholesale prices surged 4.9% y/y in April (vs 3.0% forecast), worst since 2014, driven by Iran war energy shock. Energy import prices +17.5% y/y; this signals sticky global inflation, reducing Fed rate-cut odds and pressuring equities.

factors.name: Oil Prices Elevated (~$97.50 WTI)
factors.summary: WTI holding above $97 amid Iran war and Strait of Hormuz closure concerns. Trump signaled no urgency to reopen Hormuz. Sustained high oil = persistent inflation narrative = bearish for growth equities and tech multiples.

factors.name: USD Strength (DXY >99.00)
factors.summary: US Dollar Index climbed above 99.00 for fifth consecutive day on strong data and Fed shift. Stronger USD headwinds international revenue for Nasdaq 100 components (Apple, Microsoft, Nvidia derive 40-50% revenue overseas).

factors.name: Fed Messaging Mixed/Hawkish Tilt
factors.summary: Fed Governor Barr warned shrinking balance sheet risks stability; Fed's Williams said policy is 'mildly restrictive' and he hasn't decided on June move. Market pricing suggests no near-term rate cuts; inflation expectations rising. This supports higher terminal rates, pressuring growth.

factors.name: Tech Momentum Fading; Overbought Warnings
factors.summary: MarketWatch: 'Nvidia earnings alone won't rescue the S&P 500 from its new sell signal' with overbought warnings ahead of earnings season. Record global stock rally stalled in Asia Friday. South Korea's KOSPI reversed from 8,046 record high to -2% fall.

factors.name: China Trade Deal Underwhelming
factors.summary: Trump-Xi summit yielded 200 Boeing jets (vs >500 expected), signaling deal progress but at lower magnitude. Agricultural purchases 'double-digit billions' is less dramatic than initially hyped. Market repricing risk-on narrative lower.

factors.name: Semiconductor Valuation Concerns
factors.summary: Bloomberg notes Chinese chipmakers' valuations are 'some of the most expensive among global peers,' and investors growing cautious. TSMC/Samsung supply-chain momentum slowing amid macro uncertainty.

factors.name: Equity Futures Down on Geopolitical/Inflation Fears
factors.summary: After Trump Iran comments ('losing patience'), equity futures fell sharply. Risk-off sentiment dominating despite China trade progress, reversing earlier optimism.

factors.name: Upcoming Earnings Cascade with Volatility Risk
factors.summary: NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL earnings loom; each can move NQ 2-3%. Guidance on cloud capex and AI demand critical. But pre-earnings environment historically volatile and prone to profit-taking near highs.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 faces a confluence of bearish macro headwinds: Treasury yields rising on persistent inflation (Japan PPI surprise at +4.9%), oil staying elevated on Iran war, USD strengthening, and Fed messaging suggesting rates will remain higher for longer. Tech multiples are under pressure as real yields climb and growth rate-cut bets fade. The Trump-Xi summit initially buoyed risk-on sentiment, but underwhelming deal scope (200 vs 500 Boeing jets) and Trump's hardline Iran stance have reversed momentum; South Korea's KOSPI and Asian equities fell sharply Friday. MarketWatch warns of overbought conditions and sell signals ahead of megacap earnings. While mega-cap earnings season could provide some support, the macro backdrop—inflation, higher yields, dollar strength—is actively headwind-prone for a long-duration-heavy index like Nasdaq.
catalysts_ahead: NVDA earnings (likely next week) — AI capex guidance critical
MSFT earnings (likely next week) — Azure cloud capex commentary
AAPL earnings — revenue/guidance and margin pressure from FX
GOOGL earnings — ad market, cloud growth, AI investments
US CPI/PCE data (early June expected) — inflation trajectory
FOMC June meeting (June 17-18) — rate decision and forward guidance
China SMIC/chip export control developments
Iran ceasefire talks (ongoing) — oil price sensitivity
screening.risk_warning: A soft CPI print or dovish Fed pivot would invalidate the bearish bias and trigger sharp short-covering rallies in growth. Mega-cap earnings beats with strong forward guidance (NVDA cloud demand, MSFT capex commitments) could also absorb the macro headwinds short-term. Additionally, geopolitical de-escalation (Iran ceasefire) or surprise US-China tariff rollback would flip sentiment to risk-on and lift NQ.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2 (down ~50bp from recent highs, not yet 1% extended). Wait for retest of 29700–29850 resistance or a pullback to 29300–29400 support before considering fresh shorts; Nasdaq heavily overbought ahead of earnings, making buy-the-dip risky until macro clarity improves.

factors.name: Risk-off sentiment & geopolitical escalation
factors.summary: Markets turning cautious as Trump signals 'diminishing patience' with Iran; South Korean KOSPI reversed from record 8,046 to down >2%; broad equity futures weakness across Asia-Pacific. Middle East conflict unresolved despite Trump-Xi talks.

factors.name: Oil price surge & inflation re-pricing
factors.summary: WTI rallying to $98.20+ (heading toward $98.50 per multiple sources); Japan wholesale prices up 4.9% YoY vs 3.0% expected, driven by 83.2% MoM naphtha spike. Rising energy costs push Treasury yields to near 1-year highs and compress tech multiples.

factors.name: US Treasury yields rising sharply
factors.summary: Japan govt bond yields hit record highs; global bond markets repricing higher on inflation fears and Fed shift expectations. Higher nominal yields and real yields directly compress long-duration growth valuations in NQ-heavy tech.

factors.name: US Dollar strength (DXY >99.00)
factors.summary: DXY climbing for fifth consecutive day, trading ~99.10; USD/JPY at 2-week highs; USD/CAD at 8-day winning streak. Stronger dollar headwind for Nasdaq constituents with high international revenue exposure.

factors.name: Sell-the-news Trump-Xi summit pressure
factors.summary: Despite China deal announcements (200 Boeing jets, agricultural purchases), Nasdaq futures down ~1.2% from yesterday's close; crypto also showing 'sell the news' after CLARITY Act clearing Senate. Markets repricing geopolitical/tariff uncertainty over trade wins.

factors.name: Mixed earnings tone & capex uncertainty
factors.summary: Honda +7% despite 70-year loss suggests some selective buying, but OpenAI growth capex concerns flagged; Samsung labor strike pending; broader capex/AI ROI still questioned in volatile rate environment.

factors.name: Prior SELL6/10 read unconfirmed
factors.summary: Your 2026-05-15 03:38 SELL6/10 call noted 'no confirmation triggered'; today's risk-off and Nasdaq futures weakness now providing confirmation for that earlier read.

factors.name: Overbought technical warnings
factors.summary: MarketWatch reporting overbought signals on S&P 500; mega-cap tech rallies hit headwind as long-duration valuation sensitivity to yields increases.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq rallied into record highs overnight, but sell-the-news dynamics are triggering as Trump departs China with announcements rather than tariff certainty. Geopolitical risk (Iran tensions, Hormuz strait unresolved) is now dominating, pushing oil sharply higher and repricing inflation expectations. Japan wholesale prices at 4.9% YoY (vs 3.0% expected), combined with Treasury yields spiking to near 1-year highs, are compressing the long-duration growth multiples that power the Nasdaq. The US Dollar rallying to >99.00 adds a further headwind. Asia reversing sharply (KOSPI -2% from records) signals conviction shift to risk-off; Nasdaq futures down ~1.2% suggest this is spilling into US premarket.
catalysts_ahead: US market open (premarket already showing -1.2% in NQ futures)
Retail earnings (continued flow through next 1–2 weeks)
FOMC meeting notes & Fed speakers (Barr noted no June decision yet — keeps uncertainty high)
Weekly jobless claims & NFP (labor market breadth critical)
CPI/PCE inflation data (timeline TBD, but inflation surprise risk high given oil spike)
Strait of Hormuz negotiation outcome (USTR & China ministry signaling urgency)
screening.risk_warning: If energy shock fades quickly (Hormuz reopens, oil rolls over) or Fed signals dovish pivot, real yields compress and risk-on resumes sharply — would flip bias BULLISH. Also, mega-cap earnings beats (esp. NVDA, MSFT capex guidance) could override near-term geopolitical pressure. Move is early (only ~0.14% on the day), so fast reversal on positive headlines is possible.
screening.entry_guidance: SELL on any retest of 29,700–29,800 resistance into US open; or wait for pullback to 29,400–29,500 support if momentum softens, given geopolitical/yield volatility likely to persist through end of week. Avoid chasing shorts if risk-on reversal accelerates.

factors.name: Real Yields & Treasury Curve
factors.summary: 10-year yields hit near one-year highs; 30-year broke 5% threshold earlier this week. Higher real yields compress long-duration growth multiples (tech's core sensitivity). Fed repricing toward hawkish stance on back of strong CPI/Retail Sales data.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD rallies toward best week since March on Fed hike expectations. Strong dollar headwinds for Nasdaq mega-caps (MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL, AMZN) which derive 40-60% revenue from international. EUR/USD breaks 1.1670, AUD/USD below 0.7200.

factors.name: Risk-Off Sentiment Wave
factors.summary: Multiple sources flag 'sell-the-news' risk post Trump-Xi summit; Nasdaq futures down ~1.2% in premarket per InvestingLive (06:42 UTC). Broader equities under pressure as Middle East geopolitical tensions persist; oil spike to >$100/bbl adds stagflation fears.

factors.name: AI Earnings Momentum (Narrow Rally)
factors.summary: BNY notes U.S. equities sustained by 'unusually strong AI-led earnings boom' outpacing broader economy. Pershing Square (Ackman) initiated large MSFT stake citing 'compelling' valuation. Memory stocks rally despite valuation stretch (CoinDesk: 'approaching dot-com peak valuations').

factors.name: Semiconductor Supply Chain
factors.summary: Samsung union strike plan continues (shares slide); TSMC plans Vanguard share sale; RBC raises AMAT PT on growth outlook. Mixed signals—no major shock but incremental downside risks.

factors.name: Trump-Xi Summit Resolution
factors.summary: Summit wrapped Friday with 'few deals or major breakthroughs' per Bloomberg; Xi-Trump meeting delivered 'predictable script' with little substantive trade/diplomatic progress. China trade tensions remain unresolved; no tail-risk relief catalyst materialized.

factors.name: Valuation & Froth
factors.summary: CoinDesk notes U.S. stock market approaching dot-com bubble peak valuations; real yields rising into an already stretched growth multiple environment. Tech-heavy Nasdaq increasingly vulnerable to yield re-pricing shocks.

factors.name: Geopolitical Oil Shock
factors.summary: Strait of Hormuz supply concerns (UAE accelerating bypass pipeline to 2027); oil >$100 on Trump-China deal; stagflation narrative gaining traction. Higher energy input costs threaten profit margins for growth-dependent tech names.

factors.name: Prior Bias Consistency
factors.summary: Your reads on 2026-05-15 03:38 and 06:55 both flagged SELL 6/10 (no confirmation triggered). Current risk-off wave and yield spike validate the prior bias direction, albeit with low confirmation so far.

key_levels.resistance: 29,450 (Investinglive key resistance zone); 29,600 (current vicinity); 29,800 (recent high before pullback)
key_levels.support: 29,320–29,375 (key resistance cluster now acting as dynamic support if broken); 29,000 (major technical floor); 28,800 (intermediate support)

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is caught in a post-Trump-Xi 'sell-the-news' correction amid a broader risk-off sweep. Real yields are spiking (10Y near 1-year highs, 30Y >5%), the dollar is rallying into its best week since March, and geopolitical oil shocks (Strait of Hormuz, UAE pipeline acceleration) are stoking stagflation fears. Mega-cap tech, despite strong AI earnings, faces compression of long-duration growth multiples and international revenue headwinds from USD strength. Premarket Nasdaq futures down ~1.2% signal continuation of weakness into U.S. open. The narrow AI-driven rally is increasingly isolated and vulnerable to yield shocks.
catalysts_ahead: PCE inflation data (if released next week—major Fed repricing trigger)
FOMC meeting (timing unknown, but any hawkish guidance would deepen selloff)
NVDA, MSFT, AAPL earnings announcements (likely within 2-4 weeks; forward guidance on capex critical)
U.S. non-farm payroll (NFP) Friday (if robust, reinforces Fed hike pricing)
10Y Treasury yield behavior at 5.0% psychological level (repeat breaks = accelerated NQ selloff)
China stimulus/trade policy announcement (to offset Xi summit flatness)
Middle East escalation (could spike oil further, extend stagflation/risk-off premium)
screening.risk_warning: INVALIDATION if: (1) Real yields roll over (10Y back below 4.75%) on soft inflation data; (2) Fed delivers dovish pivot signal; (3) Mega-cap earnings (MSFT, AAPL, NVDA) beat with aggressive forward capex guidance, reigniting AI narrative and justifying valuations; (4) Risk-on reversal triggered by China stimulus or geopolitical de-escalation. Current move is PHASE_2 and premarket weakness is unconfirmed by cash market open—early exit if NQ bounces hard off support.
screening.entry_guidance: Sell-the-news environment is intact but move is already in progress (NQ futures down ~1.2%). Wait for retest of 29,450–29,500 resistance cluster or a failed bounce off 29,320–29,375 support before adding shorts; avoid chasing extended moves. Alternatively, monitor 10Y yield for break above 5.0%—clean yield break is a cleaner entry for further downside (29,000 target).

factors.name: Real yields & bond selloff
factors.summary: Treasury yields jumping across curve with 30-year breaking 5% threshold; 10-year yields hit near one-year highs. Long-duration growth tech extremely sensitive to real rate compression—this is a major headwind for NQ multiples.

factors.name: Inflation concerns & Fed repricing
factors.summary: Producer costs accelerating, oil above $100 WTI on Iran tensions. Fed Governor Barr flagged inflation as 'overwhelming risk'; market pricing hawkish Fed path under new chair Kevin Warsh. This repricing is killing growth multiples.

factors.name: Oil rally & geopolitical premium
factors.summary: WTI broke above $100 on Iran war, Strait of Hormuz disruption fears, and Trump-Xi summit yielding only marginal progress. Higher energy costs are inflationary backdrop, not growth-supportive for tech-heavy Nasdaq.

factors.name: Dollar strength
factors.summary: USD rallying toward best week since March on hawkish Fed repricing; EUR/USD below 1.170, INR hits all-time low vs USD. Strong dollar headwind for NDX international revenue exposure (MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL all have significant overseas earnings).

factors.name: Equity sentiment & profit-taking risk
factors.summary: BofA strategists warn stock market 'ripe for profit-taking in early June'; Goldman Sachs note momentum/risk appetite ascendant but flagging danger ahead. Nasdaq futures showed -0.81% decline overnight (NQ to 29,450), fitting 'sell-the-news' setup post-Trump-Xi summit.

factors.name: Mega-cap tech earnings & AI narrative
factors.summary: Bill Ackman built MSFT stake on AI/cloud bet (bullish signal), but broader fund flows show AI-led earnings narrowing rally amid macro headwinds. No fresh mega-cap earnings catalyst in news; forward guidance absent.

factors.name: Valuation warning
factors.summary: CoinDesk analysis flags US market 'getting close to dot-com bubble peak valuations'; RBC's Calvasina warns 5% yields would 'challenge bullish calls.' Current 10-yr near 4.8-4.9% already testing this threshold.

factors.name: China-related dynamics
factors.summary: Trump-Xi summit concluded with Boeing deal (750 planes potential) and energy/trade alignment, but no material de-escalation on Taiwan—equities already priced in marginal progress. Semiconductor export controls remain unresolved.

factors.name: Crypto correlation & risk-off undertone
factors.summary: BTC and altcoin volatility mixed; THORChain $10M exploit causing technical distress. Crypto-NQ linkage on risk-off days suggests equities heading lower if macro deteriorates further.

key_levels.support: 29,320–29,375 (Investinglive cited as key resistance from bearish perspective)

screening.narrative: Nasdaq is caught in a classic late-cycle squeeze: equity valuations are stretched (dot-com peak territory), Treasury yields are surging on inflation fears (10-yr near 1-year highs), real yields are compressing growth multiples, and the dollar is rallying as markets reprice for hawkish Fed policy under new chair Kevin Warsh. The Trump-Xi summit delivered only marginal diplomatic progress, while oil remains elevated on Iran war risks—adding to stagflation concerns. Overnight NQ futures fell 0.81% to 29,450, and today's price action shows minimal follow-through (+0.14% in 4h, +0.22% in 24h), fitting a 'sell-the-news' pattern into key resistance 29,320–29,375. BofA and Goldman strategists are warning of profit-taking risk, especially in early June.
catalysts_ahead: Fed policy communication (new chair Warsh's first week signals/actions)
Next CPI/PCE print (timing TBD, but macro data momentum is hot)
Treasury auctions & global bond market stabilization (key for yield trajectory)
FOMC minutes or speaker commentary on inflation/rate path
Mega-cap earnings (NVDA, MSFT, AAPL guidance on capex, AI demand)
Bank of Japan intervention commentary (JPY weakness spillover risk)
Geopolitical escalation on Iran/Strait of Hormuz (oil supply shock risk)
screening.risk_warning: BULLISH INVALIDATION: (1) If CPI/PCE surprise cooler than expected → Fed pivot dovish → yields collapse → tech re-rates higher. (2) If Trump-Xi trade deal reduces tariff fears → dollar weakens → growth narrative re-emerges. (3) If Fed Warsh signals hold/cuts sooner than market prices → real yields fall → Nasdaq rallies. (4) If Nvidia/hyperscaler earnings beat on AI capex → single-name momentum could drag NQ higher despite macro headwinds. Current setup is fragile; move is underway and late to chase.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE 2 (sell-off underway, intraday volatility). For SHORT bias: wait for retest/bounce back toward 29,450–29,500 resistance zone, then re-enter with tight stop above 29,550. For LONG: only on breach above 29,600 + confirmation that yields have stabilized below 4.8%. Current price action lacks conviction; do not chase down; wait for structural shift in Fed expectations or inflation data.
